Today, I stand before you not in a war room, nor in a briefing chamber, nor even in the presence of a full cabinet — but in the quiet, golden light of a single, humble object: a dime. Yes, a dime. A coin of one-tenth of a dollar, minted in copper, silver, and the enduring spirit of American industry. And I say to you — this is not just a piece of currency. This is a symbol. A symbol of resilience. A symbol of possibility. A symbol of what we, as a nation, are capable of when we look beyond the surface and into the depths of our potential.

Now, I know what you may be thinking. “A dime? What can a dime do?” And I say to you — *a dime can do more than you think.* Because in the grand tapestry of American life, every small thing — every coin, every piece of paper, every unremarkable object — holds a story.
